feedback.text: Hello, I love playing videogames in my spare time and I would like that you included videogames preservation to the initiative. One of the most recent cases of videogames being shutdown was "The Crew", which was removed from stores and libraries by Ubisoft themself. I've never been a car guy, I enjoyed playing some racing games from time to time, but mostly with my friend, who is a big fan of racing games. This was the case until my friend told me to get The Crew, so I did and I really enjoyed my time playing it with him to the point that I started playing it by myself. I deeply loved simply driving around exploring, racing against "ghosts" of other players and collecting the various collectibles. I have not played the game in a long time and I don't believe I would again, because I already played it thoroughly and I don't want to support Ubisoft, alongside many other big corporations, but the game holds a big sentimental value for me and I feel saddened by the fact that nor I or any other person will be able to play it again as things stand right now. But this isn't the only case, another one was Global Agenda, a sci-fi MMORPG that I played with my little brother, one of the few games that we played together and just the two of us. I used to play either the Medic or Robotics, meanwhile my brother was always the Recon. Global Agenda was and still is one of the only games that I got to play as a Robotic class, which was something that, at that time, lured me into downloading it. For the biggest part of my life I only ever had small fragments of memories of the game. Until one day a couple years ago, I committed myself to finding the game that I so much loved playing. It took me a couple of hours, but once I found it and saw all those pictures, all the memories of back then came back and brought me to tears, so many good memories of the fun me and my brother had playing the game. It's something I will never forget. I really hope you consider including videogames preservation, because there are so many more games that have already been lost with no easy or even no way at all for anybody to play them, and there are plenty more that I would like to play at some point and I hope others will get a chance to play them as well. Thank you for your work and for reading my feedback.
